CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-09-2025 Text Ruling

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-18-2025 Continued To Aug-13-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay (Amended)
Off Calendar Apr-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Melanie Rodriguez, Esq. 310-432-0000 Lavi & Ebrahimian LLP for Plaintiff.
Conor Mack, Esq. 415.433.1414 ARENA HOFFMAN LLP for Defendant DEFENDANT FIVE KEYS SCHOOLS AND PROGRAMS

ARGUED. Plaintiff's counsel sent a timely notice to contest the court's tentative ruling. Ms. Rodriguez presents arguments. Mr. Mack responds and submits to the court's tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT FIVE KEYS SCHOOLS AND PROGRAMS's Motion To Stay.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FIVE KEYS SCHOOLS AND PROGRAMS's Motion To Stay.

Defendant Five Keys Schools and Programs' motion to stay based on the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction is GRANTED IN PART. The court stays this case until July 10, 2025, to enable defendant to file a motion to consolidate in the related case (Rainie S. Wooten v. Five Keys Schools and Programs, Case No. CGC-23-606322). Plaintiff Rainie S. Wooten's arguments regarding different parties (the State) and different causes of action are unpersuasive. The underlying violations in each case are the same and individual suits may result in conflicting judgments. "In keeping with both the practical nature of the rule, and the historically flexible remedial powers of equity, exactitude was not required. That the parties in the two actions 'are not entirely identical' and 'that the remedies sought by the two actions are not precisely the same' is not controlling." (Franklin & Franklin v. 7-Eleven owners for Fair Franchising (2000) 85 Cal.App.4th 1168, 1175.) Defendant did not waive the right to bring this motion by not filing a notice of related case and the court also has an interest in judicial economy and avoiding conflicting rulings. If the department presently hearing the putative class action (department 606) denies consolidation, then Defendant may renew its application for a complete stay.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-21-2025 Continued To Apr-30-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Five Keys Schools And Programs'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Number 1-39, And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,835.00
Off Calendar Mar-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Melanie Rodriguez, Esq. 310-432-0000 Lavi & Ebrahimian LLP for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - PLAINTIFF RAINIE WOOTEN's Motion To Compel Defendant Five Keys Schools And Programs'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Number 1-39, And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,835.00.

Plaintiff Ranie Wooten's motion to compel further responses to her set one document requests and for monetary sanctions is granted as to the motion to compel and denied as to the monetary sanctions. Defendant Five Keys is required to serve verified code-compliant responses and produce all non-privileged responsive documents no later than April 18, 2025. Any documents withheld on the grounds of privilege must be included in a privilege log. The parties are to engage in the Belaire-West process regarding the information of former employees no later than April 4, 2025. With the court of appeal's affirmance of the denial of the motion to compel, there is no longer any actual or arguable judicial economy reason to delay discovery. Although there are two separate cases, the parties are required to ensure that all discovery applies to both cases to avoid duplication and burdening the parties, custodians, third parties, and deponents. The discovery sought by plaintiff meets the standard for discovery relevance and Five Keys has not shown that it would be unduly burdensome to provide that discovery. Because Five Keys acted with substantial justification, no monetary sanctions are awarded.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

With the court of appeal's affirmance of the denial of the motion to compel, there is no longer any actual or arguable judicial economy reason to delay discovery.
